threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You say 'please' and 'thank you' to the bus driver, expecting a reply. Around here, you just nod and keep it moving.
Caught a glimpse of a guy in a dinosaur onesie trying to woo a girl with a ukulele on the 59 last night. Classic Brixton vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I belonged was when I found a random mural of a cat in a sombrero on a side street. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a club in a former swimming pool, complete with diving boards and an actual slide. It's a unique mix of nightlife and nostalgia!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their disdain for the Sunday farmers' market crowds but still showing up for the fresh chilaquiles.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 14 Mission, eating takeout from a carton. Only in SF do you dress sharp and keep it casual at the same time.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the squares and terraces, but it’s the narrow alleys and hidden bars that hold the real charm and community vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell them by the way they stare at the metro map—like it's a puzzle instead of just getting off at Diagonal.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot the newbies by their confusion at the bus routes; they pull out a map instead of checking the app like the rest of us.
Last night on the 47, a guy serenaded the whole bus with a keyboard app. Only in Bermondsey do you get live music at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The sound of the U-Bahn rattling beneath my feet during Sunday brunch made me realize this chaotic place felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find street art that doubles as a community fridge. Grab a snack while admiring the murals—only here!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can navigate the 14 Mission without needing Google Maps, all while timing their coffee run to avoid the morning rush.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 7 downtown today—three tourists tried to pet a dog on the tracks. The conductor's face was priceless. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think the outdoor terraces are for sipping drinks, but locals know they're just great spots for a quick siesta.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still try to order a 'café con leche' at 5 PM — locals know it's just 'café' after noon.
